name: pool-route
description: PreToolUse(Bash) pool auto-router + SessionStart routing-log snapshot, implemented in hexa-lang (`_pool_route.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). POLICY INVERSION (0.9.0 — per user directive 무조건 pool · 화이트리스트만 local 가능): the classifier now DEFAULTS every Bash command to pool dispatch. Only LOCAL-EXECUTION WHITELIST hits stay local — no more heavy-vs-light gating, no more default-local fallthrough. The whitelist (closed list, sign-gated additions only) covers cases where local execution is semantically necessary: (a) sidecar self-hooks ($CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT · $CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA literals — workstation-only cache), (b) local-mutation dispatchers (hexa cloud · hexa atlas register — write the local SSOT then commit), (c) version-control (git · gh — operate on local tree), (d) host-path commands (/Users/·/home/ abs-path heavy interp · sign-gated to prevent fork-storms), (e) per-host hidden state (~/. · $HOME/. dotstate — ~/.zsh_history, ~/.hexa-cache, ~/.pool, ~/.sidecar, …, NOT user-synced), (f) cwd-dependent installers (npm · pnpm · yarn — read package.json + write node_modules in cwd), (g) pool CLI itself (`pool on …` — the dispatcher; routing it is incoherent), (h) HOST-INTROSPECTION (0.9.0 new — ps · top · uptime · w · who · df · du · free · vmstat · iostat · uname · sysctl · launchctl · systemctl · pgrep · pkill · kill · killall · stat · lsof · netstat · ifconfig · ip · arp · route · dscl · scutil · sw_vers — these commands MEASURE THIS host's state · CPU/load/procs/disk/net/kernel/users · routing returns peer state, semantically broken for diagnostics; first-token basename match for precision), (i) `! sidecar sign local` 5-min runtime force-local token (USER-minted TUI `!` bang only · agent self-mint denied · 1 fresh token = ALL Bash local for 5min). The previous heavy_words/heavy_pairs/heavy_find_subs classifier is RETAINED for OS-capability hints (is_macos · is_linux gate which pool host) but no longer gates routing itself. Pre-0.9.0 semantics —

. The `find /` anima case that motivated 0.9.0 (root-tree scans bypassing the `find $HOME/core/`-only heavy_find_subs and running 100%+ CPU on Mac for 2:44+) now routes by default. SSH overhead trade-off accepted by policy. TERMINOLOGY (0.8.4): in pool-route context, 'WHITELIST' means LOCAL-EXECUTION WHITELIST — patterns that ESCAPE pool dispatch and stay local. The 0.8.0 phrase 'HEXA WHITELIST' was misleading (it actually defined the POOL-DISPATCH set, i.e. things that ROUTE to pool); the underlying behavior is unchanged but the canonical term is now 'HEXA POOL-DISPATCH' for the all-hexa-routes-to-pool rule, and 'LOCAL-EXECUTION WHITELIST' for things that STAY LOCAL. The TRUE whitelist (local-execution allowlist) is: (1) `$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT`/`$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA` literals (sidecar self-hooks · workstation-only cache); (2) `hexa cloud *` (itself a remote dispatcher); (3) `hexa atlas register` (writes local SSOT embedded.gen.hexa); (4) `/Users/`·`/home/` abs-path hexa (sign-gated heavy interp + path may be absent on peer); (5) `~/.`/`$HOME/.` dotstate (~/.zsh_history · ~/.hexa-cache · ~/.pool · ~/.sidecar · ~/.config · per-host hidden state, NOT user-synced); (6) `git`/`gh` commands (repository-local state); (7) `npm`/`pnpm`/`yarn` (cwd-dependent package installers); (8) `$HOME/.sidecar/signs/local.sign` fresh token via `! sidecar sign local` (5-min user-minted runtime force-local — TUI bang only, agent self-mint denied). Registration onto the LOCAL-EXECUTION WHITELIST is sign-gated by design (commons @D s13): adding hardcoded structural exemptions requires editing `_pool_route.hexa` + project.tape rule = project.tape sign-gate (`sidecar sign project`); minting the runtime force-local token requires user TUI `!` bang (`! sidecar sign local`) — no agent self-minting path exists for either. No env-var/config opt-out by design (commons @D s11). HEXA POOL-DISPATCH (0.8.0 originally named 'HEXA WHITELIST', renamed 0.8.4 — POOL-OFFLOAD m8, supersedes the heavy-verb blacklist for hexa): ANY hexa execution (`hexa`/`hexa.real`/`hexac`/`hexadrv`/`hxv2` as the real first-token verb, after env-prefix + wrapper + backslash unwrap) routes to the pool — full stop, not a per-verb blacklist. This flips the prior approach where specific heavy verbs (kick/drill/run/build/…) were whitelisted-in while verify · parse · atlas · honesty · … leaked through to the Mac and drove a multi-hundred-process clang/atlas-parse fork-storm. Now everything hexa is offloaded; only 4 STRUCTURAL exemptions stay local (handled earlier in main, never reaching the whitelist): (1) `$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT`/`$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA` hexa (sidecar's own hooks/commands — routing the router is incoherent + the plugin cache is workstation-only); (2) `hexa cloud` (itself a remote dispatcher); (3) `/Users/`·`/home/`·`~/.` absolute-path hexa (sign-gated — path may be absent on the peer); (4) `hexa atlas register` (0.8.1 — mutates the local SSOT embedded.gen.hexa then shares via local git commit; routing it writes the peer's atlas + leaves the local file unchanged → broken share — only the `register` WRITE subverb is pinned, read forms still route). Preflight fix (0.8.2): the per-host hexa readiness probe DROPPED the obsolete `test -x ~/core/hexa-lang/build/hexa_interp` gate — hexa is a COMPILED toolchain and the tree-walking interpreter (hexa_interp) is DEPRECATED, so probing for it wrongly SKIPPED hosts with a perfectly working compiled hexa (ubu-2 had hexa on PATH + hexa_cli_driver + hexa_v2 but no leftover interp → was rejected; ubu-1 only passed on a stale leftover). Readiness now = `command -v hexa` + a cheap real `hexa <verb> --help` (exercises the live compile path), no build-dir-artifact dependence. Pool-canonical canon (0.8.3): pool dispatch now routes hexa through `~/.hx/canon` — a pool-owned origin/main checkout SEPARATE from each host's `~/core/hexa-lang` (which often carries a feature branch, behind state, or anima WIP). The host wrapper at `~/.hx/bin/hexa` reads `HEXA_REAL_BIN`, so injecting it + `HEXA_LANG=$HOME/.hx/canon` + `HEXA_DAEMON=0` pins both the binary AND stdlib to origin/main without touching the user's dev tree. Bootstrapped by `~/.hx/canon-update.sh` on each host (idempotent: git fetch origin main + reset --hard + copy bootstrap C from ~/core/hexa-lang + hexa cc --regen → ~/.hx/canon/build/hexa_linux + .last_update timestamp). Solves the stale-compiler / qwen_bpe miscompile class (ubu-2 was 614 commits behind origin/main on a stale main + DNS-broken so git fetch impossible; canon is independent of that drift). Readiness probe now: `test -x ~/.hx/canon/build/hexa_linux` AND `command -v hexa` AND wrapper executes `hexa --help` THROUGH canon. Hosts missing canon fail preflight with a deny-trailer naming canon-update.sh. Auto-self-heal on stale deferred to 0.8.4 (probe must stay fast; user runs `pool on <host> ~/.hx/canon-update.sh` to refresh). The legacy heavy_words/heavy_pairs classifier REMAINS for NON-hexa heavy compute (make · cargo · gcc · node · java · ffmpeg · …). When the `pool` CLI's roster (`~/.pool/pool.json`) has ≥1 enabled host and a Bash command matches the hexa whitelist or the (non-hexa) heavy classifier (make · cargo · npm · gradle · pytest · go build · swift build · docker build · gcc/g++/clang/clang++/cc/c++ · nvidia-smi · train · hexa kick/drill/loop/cc/run/build · aprime_cc · `find $HOME/core/anima` big-tree scan · …) or a root-needing command, the command is rewritten via the hook `updatedInput` field to run on a pool host over ssh (`ssh <host> 'cd <workdir> && <cmd>'`) — transparent dispatch, not a suggestion. Routing is EXPLICIT-classifier only (0.6.0): the 0.5.7 load-escalation gate — which promoted any non-trivial cmd to heavy when summed non-claude CPU exceeded 150% — was removed because on a multi-core Mac that threshold trips constantly and shipped local-bound git/hexa-run/smoke commands to Linux hosts where their paths don't exist. Local-bound guard (0.6.0, extended 0.6.9): `git`/`gh` commands, any `/Users/`·`/home/` absolute-path literal, and any `~/.`·`$HOME/.` home-dotstate reference always run local — the dotstate forms (`~/.zsh_history`·`~/.hexa-cache`·`~/.pool`·`~/.sidecar`·`~/.config`·…) are per-host hidden state that is NOT user-synced, so routing them to a pool host reads the WRONG host's history/cache; `~/core/...` synced workdirs are excluded and keep routing (the `find ~/core/anima` big-tree pattern still dispatches). Sidecar plugin pin (0.6.8): cmds carrying `$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT` or `$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA` literals always run local — the literals resolve via the dispatching bash to the workstation's plugin cache, which only exists on the local Mac; routing them to a pool host fails (cache absent). Trusted surface (sidecar slash commands like `/quota:quota`, `/pool:pool`, `/check`, `/end`, `/ship`, `/inject`, `/secret`, … all match by writing convention @D s3), so no sign gate. Local-bound sign gate (0.6.4): within the absolute-path exemption, HEAVY invocations (hexa CLI any verb · direct `hexa.real` · python/python3/py · gcc/g++/clang/clang++/cc/c++ · `bash`/`sh <script>`) now require a fresh `local` sign-off token — they were the canonical mac fork-storm trigger (multi-session dispatch-cache races + re-fork on every env-hash miss). USER mints with `! sidecar sign local` in the TUI prompt (5min token, mints freely via `!` bang); the token file `~/.sidecar/signs/local.sign` holds the mint epoch. Without a fresh token the hook emits `permissionDecision: deny` with an instruction trailer. Bare absolute-path reads (ls/cat/grep/find/...) stay free — the gate narrows to interpreters/compilers only. HOTFIX 0.6.5: (a) `_local_heavy_interp` now matches FIRST-TOKEN basename instead of `_has_word("hexa")` — 0.6.4 mis-matched `hexa` inside path strings like `/Users/.../hexa-lang/foo.txt`, gating bare reads like `cat <path>`. (b) `hexa run` + `hexa build` added to the heavy classifier — 0.6.4 missed them, so they ran local on the mac instead of routing to the pool (= mac fork-storm trigger when multi-session). The two together close the false-positive AND false-negative paths. macOS pool-host enable (0.6.6 — supersedes 0.6.1 Zero-macOS-offload): macOS pool hosts (e.g. `mini` with `os: macos`) are now AUTO-DISPATCH candidates for macOS-only commands (swift build · xcodebuild · codesign · …). The 0.6.1 blanket exclusion left compile-heavy work (swift-frontend can drive 85%+ CPU on the workstation) stuck on the local Mac even when an enabled `mini` was idle. New policy: (a) macOS-only commands route to macOS pool hosts; (b) Linux-only commands route to Linux pool hosts (unchanged); (c) general-heavy commands round-robin across Linux pool hosts only — the workstation Mac stays protected from generic build/test fan-out and is reached only when a macOS-capability marker explicitly demands it. Heavy-classified commands MUST use pool resources: the hook iterates every eligible host through a preflight (workdir present + the command's toolchain actually runnable — `command -v hexa` + `test -x build/hexa_interp` + `hexa <verb> --help` for hexa, `command -v <compiler>` for C/C++ — 0.6.13), routes to the first that passes, and if ALL fail emits a `permissionDecision: deny` with a per-host failure breakdown — never silently falls back to local. `hexa kick`/`drill`/`loop`/`cc` get `HEXA_LANG=$HOME/core/hexa-lang`; other hexa commands get `HEXA_LANG=$PWD`. Each routing decision appends one JSON line to `~/.pool/route-log.jsonl` (capped at 100); SessionStart reads the last 5 back as additionalContext. Cloud pin (0.6.10): `hexa cloud *` always runs local — it is ITSELF a Mac-local-only remote-dispatch tool (needs the local hexa build + stdlib/cloud), and its post-`--` remote argv routinely carries heavy words (nvidia-smi · train.log · make) that tripped the classifier, non-deterministically shipping `hexa cloud exec <pod> -- ...` to a Linux pool host with no `cloud` subcommand. Double-routing a dispatcher is incoherent. Worktree→canonical-root fallback (0.6.10): a heavy command in a git LINKED worktree outside $HOME (e.g. /tmp/wt-x — the standard stdlib/SSCHA isolation pattern) previously DENIED ('cwd outside $HOME'); it now resolves the MAIN checkout via `git worktree list --porcelain` (listed first) and, when that root is under $HOME (a synced ~/core workdir), mirrors it instead. This runs ONLY in the previously-denying branch, so it cannot regress a routing command — it strictly rescues worktree dispatch. C/C++ compiler routing (0.6.11): the C/C++ compiler drivers (`gcc` · `g++` · `clang` · `clang++` · `cc` · `c++`) join the heavy classifier — a C/C++ compile is heavy local compute, same workstation-protection rationale as `make`/`cmake`/`cargo`. They carry no macOS-capability marker, so they fall into the general-heavy bucket → Linux-only round-robin (the Mac stays the workstation, per @D s12). Word-matched (not first-token) so a zsh-snapshot-wrapped invocation is still caught; `cc` is the only 2-char driver but git/gh + absolute-path commands already returned local above, so a coincidental bare `cc` token is unlikely to reach the classifier. Absolute-path compiler sign gate (0.6.12): the C/C++ compiler drivers also join the local-bound sign gate within the absolute-path exemption — an absolute-path compile (e.g. `/usr/bin/gcc /Users/…/x.c`) is the same mac fork-storm trigger as a heavy interpreter, so it now requires a fresh `local` sign-off token (`! sidecar sign local`) just like hexa/python/sh; relative-path compiles in synced workdirs still route to the pool unchanged. First-token basename match keeps it precise — only a real compiler invocation, never a bare read mentioning `cc` inside a path. Toolchain preflight + akida exclusion (0.6.13): (a) pi5-akida is excluded from the auto-dispatch candidate set entirely (@D s8 — anima-only; reached ONLY via explicit `pool on pi5-akida`, the pool CLI not this hook). (b) the host preflight now verifies the command's ACTUAL toolchain, not just the workdir — every `hexa` verb probes `command -v hexa` + `test -x ~/core/hexa-lang/build/hexa_interp` + `hexa <verb> --help`, and a C/C++ compile probes `command -v <compiler>`; a host that fails is SKIPPED so the round-robin moves to the next eligible host instead of routing-then-failing (the ubu-1 broken-hexa trap). Known limit (@D d6): a binary that passes `--help` but segfaults at runtime is not caught (full exec probe deferred). mini-equal toggle (0.7.0 — supersedes the 0.6.6 Linux-only-general policy when opted in): `pool mini-equal on` writes `~/.pool/mini-equal = "on"`, which opts macOS pool hosts (mini) INTO the OS-agnostic general-heavy round-robin as equal workers alongside the Linux hosts. Reached ONLY in the general-heavy branch (OS-agnostic work) — is_linux commands (apt · elf · CUDA) still route Linux-only and never land on a macOS host, so the toggle only redistributes portable work. Default OFF (marker absent or != "on") preserves zero-macOS-offload (@D s12). This is NOT an in-guard opt-out (@D s11): it OPENS routing to an otherwise-idle host, it does not DISABLE a safety guard. Single local-execution gate (0.7.2): a fresh `local` sign token (`! sidecar sign local`, 5min) now forces EVERY command to run local — the one deliberate, user-minted 'run here, do NOT route' path, checked at the top of the router before classification. Consolidates local-exec control to one sign-gated channel: when the router mis-classifies a local-bound command (a diagnostic `ps`/`awk`, a local-fs fix) and ships it to a peer host, the user signs ONCE and all routing is suppressed for the window. Subsumes the abs-path-heavy sign gate (when signed, the top-level check returns first; when unsigned, that gate still bites — fork-storm protection unchanged). A sanctioned, time-boxed, user-minted token — NOT an env-var/config opt-out (@D s11). Env-prefix + wrapper hardening (0.7.3): `_local_heavy_interp` now strips POSIX env-var prefix (`POOL_DISABLE=1`/`SIDECAR_POOL_DISABLE=1`/`FOO=bar`/…, any IDENT=val — not specific vars, parser correctness only) AND known leading-position wrappers (`env` · `exec` · `nice` · `timeout` · `command` · `sudo` · `nohup` · `stdbuf` · `ionice`, with per-wrapper option-arg awareness — `nice -n 10`/`env -u VAR`/`timeout -k 5 60`/`stdbuf -oL`/…) AND a leading backslash escape (`\hexa`) before reading the real verb. Pre-0.7.3 any env-var prefix OR any of those wrappers silently ALLOW'd abs-path heavy invocations, breaking sign-local's single-gate integrity (verified leak across 22 vectors; all closed post-fix). `eval 'quoted shell string'` remains out of scope (its arg requires a real shell parser). Node-package installer pin (0.7.4): npm/pnpm/yarn run LOCAL — they read package.json + write node_modules in CWD, so routing them to a pool host (where the CWD usually doesn't exist) failed preflight on every host and denied the command. Removed from heavy_words and added to the local-bound early-_allow chain. Routing-rate baseline counters (0.7.5 — POOL-OFFLOAD m1): every meaningful classifier verdict increments one of 4 counters in `~/.pool/route-counters.tally` (routed · local_bound · light_allowed · heavy_failed) so `/check` can report the routing-rate baseline (% of bash invocations actually offloaded to the pool vs stayed local). Non-classifier exits are NOT counted. Race-tolerant under concurrent sessions. Useless env-prefix advisory (0.7.11 — agent education): when SIDECAR_NO_POOL / SIDECAR_NO_POOL_ROUTE / POOL_DISABLE / SIDECAR_POOL_DISABLE env-prefix is detected at the head of a Bash cmd, the hook emits an inline `additionalContext` advisory explaining these are NO-OP since 0.7.3 (POSIX env-prefix stripped before classifier) and that `! sidecar sign local` is the only legitimate local-force path. Routing behavior is UNCHANGED — advisory is purely educational so the agent stops re-trying the same useless prefix silently. Module-level `_ADVISORY` mutable threads through `_allow_count` / `_deny_count` / `_emit` exits. big-tree find generalization (0.7.10 — POOL-OFFLOAD m5): `heavy_find_subs` generalized from single `find $HOME/core/anima` to BOTH `find <HOME>/core/` (expanded) AND `find ~/core/` (tilde form). Routes ANY `~/core/*` sibling repo find — anima/hexa-lang/demiurge/phanes/sidecar/... — instead of just anima. Preflight test -d still gates each host (non-mirrored hosts auto-skip). Small repos pay marginal SSH overhead; the Mac stays clean. Media tools (0.7.9 — POOL-OFFLOAD m4): heavy_words +2 (`ffmpeg`/`magick` — 6-char specific media tools) + abs-path first-token sign-gate +4 (ffmpeg/magick/convert/sox). `convert`/`sox` are first-token-only (English-word substring-collide). JVM compilers + sbt (0.7.8 — POOL-OFFLOAD m3): heavy_words +3 (`javac`/`kotlinc`/`scalac` — 5-7 char specific compilers) + heavy_pairs +4 (`java -jar`/`sbt compile`/`sbt test`/`sbt run` — pair-only for short ambiguous names) + `_local_heavy_interp` first-token +5 (java/javac/kotlinc/scalac/sbt sign-gate). mvn/gradle already in. Node ecosystem interpreters (0.7.7 — POOL-OFFLOAD m2): heavy_words +4 (`node`/`deno`/`bun`/`ts-node`) + `_local_heavy_interp` first-token basename match adds the same set + `tsx` (safe at first-token layer; excluded from heavy_words due to `.tsx` file-extension collision via `_has_word`). Mirrors python/hexa fork-storm protection for JS/TS runtimes; npm/pnpm/yarn stay LOCAL (0.7.4 cwd-dependent). Heavy hexa verb sweep (0.7.6 — POOL-OFFLOAD m6): heavy_pairs extended +19 verbs from the hexa 80+ catalog — toolchain (`hexa test` · `hexa bench`); math verifiers (`hexa absolute` Mk.VIII · `hexa meta-closure` Mk.IX); math discovery (`hexa smash` 9-phase singularity drill · `hexa free` DFS compose · `hexa hyperarithmetic` Π₀² 5-system); discovery extras (`hexa chain` L3 cross-engine); 11 drill variants (omega · surge · dream · swarm · reign · molt · wake · forge · canon · debate · revive — each composes drill with a different orchestration pattern, all fork-storm class). Excluded as light (read/lookup only): atlas hash/stats/lookup/dump/verify · 29 annotation analyzers · 16 HTTP data bridges · lattice verify · gpu lint/disasm. Excluded as deferred (3-token pair needs richer classifier): hexa gpu fire · hexa sim-universe <X> · hexa qmirror <X> · hexa atlas register --from-drill. OS-capability filter + round-robin load balance. NO guard opt-out by design.
